name: Burnt Sugar Ice Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the sugar in a heavy saucepan over medium-high heat, stirring constantly, until it turns dark brown.
2. Meanwhile, combine the cream and milk in a saucepan and heat until the mixture bubbles around the edge.
3. When the sugar has caramelized, remove from heat and slowly begin stirring in the hot milk mixture. Be careful; the mixture will bubble violently at first. Stop pouring and stir until the bubbling subsides, then continue pouring until all of the milk is incorporated and the caramel has completely dissolved.
4. Place egg yolks in a bowl, add salt, and beat to combine.
5. Slowly stir in half of the hot milk mixture into the yolks.
6. Pour back into the remaining hot milk and cook over moderate heat, stirring constantly, until the custard thickens lightly.
7. Remove from heat and stir in brandy and vanilla.
8. Refrigerate until completely chilled. Freeze in an ice cream machine according to manufacturer's directions.
9. Serves 4.
PER SERVING: 460 calories, 7 g protein, 40 g carbohydrate, 30 g fat (17 saturated), 305 mg cholesterol, 108 mg sodium, 0 g fiber.
